rationales.tradition: She works within inherited routines and lineage, using the same parish-hall setup and trying to capture the river "the way Grand-mere did in her later work."
She preserves family and community continuity through the "seventh year running" menu, Grand-mere's molding profile, and wanting to ask Maman before including the 1988 sketch.

rationales.self_direction: She resists outside pressure from the gallerist and decides for herself that she already knows the answer, rather than letting a more prestigious path steer her.
rationales.tradition: She rejects the Brussels opportunity because the parish-hall show connects to neighbors who remember Grand-mere's work "on those same walls," and that continuity matters more to her.

rationales.security: She experiences changing the Sunday lunches as threatening, imagining "empty chairs" and the table "too big," which shows a strong pull toward keeping family life predictable and intact.
rationales.tradition: She insists that "three generations of Sunday lunches" and her grandfather's deliberate choice to build the long table "deserve to be honored even when they stop being convenient."

rationales.stimulation: She dismisses the suggestion to try a tablet and explicitly rejects "whatever's newest," favoring inherited methods over novelty or experimentation.
rationales.tradition: She treats painting the same river in the same medium as her grandmother and mother as a way to keep "the line of people who practiced it before me" alive.
The test hang matters because it recreates the same light, hour, and wall as Grand-mere's 1978 piece, showing satisfaction in continuity with an older family pattern.

rationales.hedonism: She lets her own comfort and dinner go cold, saying she stayed past her shift and gave up her evening rather than prioritize rest or pleasure.
rationales.benevolence: She stays after a twelve-hour shift to explain treatment in Arabic, fill out forms, and sit with "a terrified mother," clearly putting another family's immediate needs ahead of her own.

rationales.tradition: She anchors the day in family customs and inherited wisdom, from making mansaf for everyone to reflecting on what her mother "used to say" about love and care.
rationales.benevolence: She frames her work and home life around caring for her family, wanting her home to be "always open, always warm" and teaching her children that love means showing up when tired.
Even off shift, she returns because Ahmed's mother asked for her, holds her while she screams, and describes having chosen "to love other people's children as my own."
She interrupts her studio work to bus over and feed Ma congee, then judges the day by whether she "showed up where it counted," centering care for close family.

rationales.security: Her first response to Yuwen's move is to ask about "salary," "housing," and "health insurance," and she is shaken by how "quiet it will be" once her daughter is gone, showing concern with stability and future safety.

rationales.self_direction: She deliberately paints with "no plan, no composition sketch" and values the feeling of working without a destination, reclaiming creative autonomy she had neglected.
rationales.benevolence: She calls Yuwen back to give the support she couldn't give the night before, and then brings steamed buns to Xiaoli and makes time to sit with her.
She patiently guides a child's hand through writing her name and values the studio being full of "eight small people," finding meaning in showing up for them rather than only finishing her own work.
She hesitates to accept a good professional opportunity because "nobody checks if Ma ate her congee," prioritizing close family care over personal exposure.

rationales.security: After Ma's fall, her focus narrows to safety and stability, repeating that "she is okay and that is the only thing that matters right now."
rationales.benevolence: She stays all day, reads to Ma, makes her favorite soup, and watches her breathe, showing sustained hands-on care for a close family member.
When Xiaoli calls crying, she immediately says "we will figure it out together" and plans to take more morning shifts with Ma so her sister is not carrying it alone.

rationales.security: Hiring Ah-Fen for "three mornings a week" creates a more dependable care arrangement and lets both sisters "breathe a little," directly improving household stability.
rationales.benevolence: She arranges support around Ma's comfort, insisting on a trial week and noting with relief that Ma was soon sharing family photos and seemed comfortable.
The visit centers on closeness with Yuwen before she leaves, and painting together "for the first time since she was a teenager" highlights nurturing a cherished family bond.
She treasures "everyone at one table" and defines her life through "making sure the people around me are okay," expressing deep attachment and care for close family as Yuwen prepares to leave.

rationales.achievement: She actively makes March feasible by telling Ah-Fen she may need to do "a full weekend," taking concrete steps so she can participate in Mr. Tsai's show.
rationales.security: The entry is organized around a reliable support structure: groceries, family check-ins, weekend coverage, and Sunday calls, leading her to realize that "some of them can hold themselves."
